title: leucas dimidiata
intro: Leucas dimidiata, also known as the fragrant-leaved leucas, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used in various parts of the world, particularly in India and Sri Lanka, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Leucas dimidiata include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and aiding in digestion. Leucas dimidiata is often used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and poultices, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
